campus.name: Miami Campus
campus.description: One of AASTMT’s flagship Alexandria campuses, focused on business education, applied research, industry engagement, and international accreditation.

college.name: College of Management & Technology – Miami
college.parent_institution: Arab Academy for Science, Technology and Maritime Transport
college.positioning: AACSB-accredited business college emphasizing applied management education, technology integration, quality assurance, and industry-driven learning.
college.vision: To be recognized as a premier business college in higher education and applied research, serving industry and society at the local, regional, and international levels.
college.mission: To provide high-quality management education and applied research through modern teaching methodologies, strong ethical values, and continuous engagement with industry and society.

national: National Authority for Quality Assurance and Accreditation of Education (NAQAAE)
international: Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB)

quality_assurance.description: Comprehensive quality assurance framework aligned with AACSB, ISO 9001:2015, and NAQAAE standards to ensure continuous improvement and academic excellence.

programs.name: Business Information Systems
programs.focus: Business systems, data management, decision support, and IT-enabled organizations

programs.name: Finance and Accounting
programs.focus: Financial reporting, corporate finance, auditing, and financial analysis

programs.name: Marketing and International Business
programs.focus: Marketing strategy, international trade, and global business environments

programs.name: Media Management
programs.focus: Media economics, management, and strategic communication

programs.name: Hotels and Tourism Management
programs.focus: Hospitality operations, tourism economics, and service management

programs.name: Political Science
programs.focus: Public policy, governance, and political economy

programs.name: Francophone Management Program
programs.focus: Business and management education delivered in French

best_for: Students interested in applied and industry-linked learning
Students aiming for corporate and multinational careers
Students preferring structured but practical programs
less_suitable_for: Students seeking purely theoretical economics programs
Students avoiding teamwork and applied coursework

vice_deans: Vice Dean for Environment & International Affairs
